title: Highway Maintenance Workers
description: Maintain highways, municipal and rural roads, airport runways, and rights-of-way. Duties include patching broken or eroded pavement and repairing guard rails, highway markers, and snow fences. May also mow or clear brush from along road, or plow snow from roadway.

tasks.text: Set out signs and cones around work areas to divert traffic.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Flag motorists to warn them of obstacles or repair work ahead.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform preventative maintenance on vehicles and heavy equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Drive trucks to transport crews and equipment to work sites.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Erect, install, or repair guardrails, road shoulders, berms, highway markers, warning signals, and highway lighting, using hand tools and power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean and clear debris from culverts, catch basins, drop inlets, ditches, and other drain structures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive heavy equipment and vehicles with adjustable attachments to sweep debris from paved surfaces, mow grass and weeds, remove snow and ice, and spread salt and sand.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Haul and spread sand, gravel, and clay to fill washouts and repair road shoulders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect, clean, and repair drainage systems, bridges, tunnels, and other structures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Remove litter and debris from roadways, including debris from rock and mud slides.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dump, spread, and tamp asphalt, using pneumatic tampers, to repair joints and patch broken pavement.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform roadside landscaping work, such as clearing weeds and brush, and planting and trimming trees.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Apply poisons along roadsides and in animal burrows to eliminate unwanted roadside vegetation and rodents.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure and mark locations for installation of markers, using tape, string, or chalk.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Paint traffic control lines and place pavement traffic messages, by hand or using machines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Apply oil to road surfaces, using sprayers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect markers to verify accurate installation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Place and remove snow fences used to prevent the accumulation of drifting snow on highways.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Blend compounds to form adhesive mixtures used for marker installation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
